title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
After parting ways with an acquaintance at number 61 Albany Road, the narrator walks toward Wilbraham Crescent, reflecting on the lack of witnesses regarding the death of Mr. Corry (Castleton). The narrator mentally lists several unresolved questions, including when Corry was anesthetized, where he was murdered, and how his body arrived at number 19. The narrator considers visiting Miss Pemaroo, Miss Waterhouse, or Mrs. Humble, noting that Mrs. Humble’s tangential speech might contain useful information.

Upon arriving at number 19, the narrator finds the street desolate and quiet. After a brief, futile attempt to converse with an orange tabby cat sitting on the doorpost of number 20, the narrator observes that the neighborhood lacks the "gossipy" residents typically helpful to an investigation, as many elderly people have moved to nursing homes or hospitals.

While observing the large, modern apartment block, the narrator sees a flash of light from a third-floor window. Using a small bird-watching telescope, the narrator identifies a child with a long braid looking out from the window. After an old Rolls-Royce passes by, the narrator approaches the apartment building. Avoiding the empty doorman station, the narrator takes the elevator to the third floor. Finding door number 77, the narrator decides the number seven is lucky and presses the doorbell to see who responds.
